<Meta of={TileItemStories} name="Overview" />

# Tile Item

The tile item component is a versatile card-style element used within tile grids to display content in an organized, scannable format. It supports three presentation styles (heading, icon, text-only) and can include optional background images, making it ideal for creating visual grids of information, statistics, or navigational elements.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the tile item's properties in real-time.

<Canvas of={TileItemStories.TileItem} />
<Controls of={TileItemStories.TileItem} />

        <code>tile__item__content</code>

      <td>
        <code>tile__item__animation</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>'false'</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        Enable hover animation: <code>'true'</code> or <code>'false'</code>

    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>tile__item__icon_name</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>

        Icon identifier (used with 'icon' presentation style, e.g.,

      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Presentation Styles

### Heading

Displays a large, prominent heading (typically a number or short text) with supporting content below. Ideal for statistics, facts, or figures.

**Use for:**

- Statistics and metrics (e.g., "1,500 Students")
- Key numbers and data points
- Facts and figures displays
- Impact statements

**Visual characteristics:**

- Large, bold heading text
- Smaller descriptive content below
- Clear hierarchy between heading and content
- Emphasis on the numeric or heading value

### Icon

Features a decorative icon above the content text. Perfect for categorized information or feature highlights.

**Use for:**

- Feature lists with visual indicators
- Service offerings or capabilities
- Category navigation
- Process steps with icons

**Visual characteristics:**

- Icon displayed prominently at top
- Content text below icon
- Visual interest through iconography
- Clear categorical representation

### Text-Only

Displays only the content text without additional visual elements. Clean and minimal approach.

**Use for:**

- Simple text grids
- Quote collections
- Testimonial displays
- Minimalist designs

**Visual characteristics:**

- Clean, text-focused design
- No decorative elements
- Maximum space for content
- Emphasis on readability

## Alignment Options

### Horizontal Alignment

- **Left**: Content aligns to the left edge (default, best for readability)
- **Center**: Content centered within the tile (good for symmetrical layouts)
- **Right**: Content aligns to the right edge (use sparingly for visual variety)

### Vertical Alignment

- **Top**: Content positioned at the top of the tile (default, provides consistency)
- **Bottom**: Content positioned at the bottom (useful with background images)

## Background Images

Tiles can include background images that appear behind the content:

- Adds visual interest and context
- Works well with vertical alignment options
- Should have sufficient contrast with text
- Optional overlay ensures text readability
- Supports responsive image formats

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-tile-item.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-tile-item.scss`
- **Parent Component**: Used within Tiles (organism)

### Accessibility

- **Semantic HTML**: Proper list structure (`<li>` element)
- **Clickable tiles**: Entire tile is keyboard accessible when linked
- **ARIA labels**: Descriptive labels for screen reader users
- **Color contrast**: Text meets WCAG AA standards against backgrounds
- **Focus indicators**: Clear keyboard focus states
- **Icon accessibility**: Icons include appropriate aria-hidden and text alternatives

### Grid Context

Tile items are designed to be used within a tiles container that controls the grid layout:

- **Three column grid**: Standard layout (default)
- **Responsive behavior**: Adjusts columns based on viewport size
- **Equal height tiles**: All tiles in a row maintain consistent height
- **Gap spacing**: Consistent spacing between tiles

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use consistent presentation styles within a single tile grid
- Keep content concise (1-2 sentences maximum)
- Ensure sufficient contrast between text and background images
- Use icons that clearly represent the content category
- Apply themes consistently across related tiles
- Make entire tiles clickable when they represent links
- Test readability with and without background images
- Choose vertical alignment based on image composition

### Things to Avoid

- Don't mix too many presentation styles in one grid
- Avoid overly long content that causes uneven tile heights
- Don't use background images that obscure text
- Avoid using too many different icons in one grid
- Don't rely solely on color to convey meaning
- Avoid making some tiles clickable and others not in the same grid
- Don't use center alignment unless the entire grid is centered
- Avoid animations on every tile (can be overwhelming)

### Content Recommendations

**Heading style content:**

- "1,500" / "Students enrolled"
- "95%" / "Satisfaction rate"
- "24/7" / "Support available"

**Icon style content:**

- Brief feature descriptions
- Service categories
- Process step descriptions

**Text-only style content:**

- Short quotes or testimonials
- Simple statements
- Brief descriptions

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all presentation styles, alignments, and theme combinations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-tile-item-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Tiles (Organism)**: The container component that houses tile items
- **Facts and Figures**: Specialized version for displaying statistics
- **Cards**: Alternative card-style component for more complex content
